content_of_post_to_comment:
Hello. I hope someone can help me with this.

I'm young (15 years old) and have big plans. I'm passionate about video games, programming, 3D modeling and animation, and music. Basically, I'd like to create a game. The problem? I've done all the preparations. I watched a few tutorials on YouTube, but I didn't understand them, so I figured I'd learn on my own, working with my rig. I got some 3D models to use temporarily, and I bought some virtual instruments for $40 on sale (they normally cost $100). In short, I'm ready. But I procrastinate, and not just a little: despite my preparations, I've only launched Unreal Engine five times, and Blender twice just to do nothing (and again, I did nothing). I've never opened FL Studio, and it's been like this for a year and a half. I scroll through YouTube, I read manhwa (digital comics), and I keep going. But even when I have nothing to do, I'm bored, or I want to open Unreal Engine, I don't. And right now, I don't think it's procrastination because I don't tell myself, "I'll do it later," but "I'm fed up." The interface is incredibly discouraging, the controls aren't intuitive (clicking the mouse wheel to change the viewing angle, seriously?), I only see the negatives. Maybe I've chosen the wrong career path? I love imagining worlds and all sorts of things, so maybe I should submit some writing? I need help because, currently, my days seem extremely slow, to the point that I've found myself a small online volunteer job, but even that is difficult for me (I only stay because I do the bare minimum of "work," nothing more).
comment: The part about opening Unreal only five times in a year and a half sounds less like choosing the wrong path and more like being stuck at the "starting" stage. A lot of creative tools feel awful before you have enough familiarity to make anything. Maybe try making something ridiculously small, like a room, a simple animation, or a tiny prototype, instead of carrying the weight of "I need to create a game."
